quizzes.title: General Knowledge
quizzes.description: Test your basic understanding of geography, animals, history, and general facts around the globe.

questions.question: What is the capital of India?
questions.explanation: New Delhi is the official capital of India.

questions.question: Which is the largest planet in our solar system?
questions.explanation: Jupiter is the largest planet, with a mass more than two and a half times that of all the other planets combined.

questions.question: Which is the fastest land animal?
questions.explanation: The cheetah can reach speeds of between 80 and 128 km/h, making it the fastest land animal.

questions.question: Which is the largest ocean on Earth?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest of Earth's oceanic divisions.

questions.question: What is the currency of Japan?
questions.explanation: The Japanese Yen (JPY) is the official currency of Japan.

questions.question: In which country is Mount Everest located?
questions.explanation: Mount Everest lies on the border between Nepal and Tibet (China), with the peak itself in Nepal.

questions.question: How many continents are there on Earth?
questions.explanation: Earth is conventionally divided into seven continents: Asia, Africa, North America, South America, Antarctica, Europe, and Australia.

questions.question: What common liquid is chemically represented by H2O?
questions.explanation: Water is a chemical compound consisting of two hydrogen atoms bonded to a single oxygen atom.

questions.question: What is the national animal of India?
questions.explanation: The Royal Bengal Tiger is the national animal of India.

questions.question: Which direction does the sun rise from?
questions.explanation: The Earth rotates from west to east, making the sun appear to rise in the east.

quizzes.title: React.js Basics
quizzes.description: Test your fundamentals of React, hooks, states, virtual DOM, and component state management.

questions.question: Which company actively maintains React.js?
questions.explanation: React was originally created by Jordan Walke, a software engineer at Meta (formerly Facebook), and is still maintained by them.

questions.question: Which hook is used to manage local state in a functional component?
questions.explanation: useState is the built-in Hook that allows you to add state variables to functional components.

questions.question: What does JSX stand for?
questions.explanation: JSX stands for JavaScript XML, which allows us to write HTML elements in JavaScript files.

questions.question: What is the default file extension used for React components containing JSX?
questions.explanation: While regular .js works in some setups, .jsx is the conventional React file extension.

questions.question: Which of the following is the standard library used for routing in React?
questions.explanation: React Router is a fully-featured client-side routing library for React.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the Virtual DOM in React?
questions.explanation: The Virtual DOM optimizes rendering performance by only updating actual DOM elements that have changed.

questions.question: In React, component state should be treated as:
questions.explanation: React state should be treated as immutable; you should never mutate state directly, but rather use the state setter function.

questions.question: Which Hook is used to handle side effects in React functional components?
questions.explanation: useEffect lets you synchronize a component with an external system (fetching data, setting timers, direct DOM updates).

questions.question: Props passed down to a child component are:
questions.explanation: Props are read-only to ensure a predictable one-way data flow from parent to child.

questions.question: Which modern build tool is standard for scaffolding React apps with rapid hot module replacement (HMR)?
questions.explanation: Vite is a fast build tool and dev server that has largely replaced Webpack for modern React project templates.

quizzes.title: Movies & Entertainment
quizzes.description: Test your knowledge on popular blockbusters, superhero universes, books, and iconic pop culture facts.

questions.question: Which superhero universe does Iron Man belong to?
questions.explanation: Iron Man is a Marvel Comics character, played by Robert Downey Jr. in the Marvel Cinematic Universe.

questions.question: Who directed the blockbuster movie 'Avatar'?
questions.explanation: James Cameron directed Avatar, Titanic, and Aliens.

questions.question: Who wrote the famous Harry Potter fantasy book series?
questions.explanation: J.K. Rowling is the British author who created the wizarding world of Harry Potter.

questions.question: Who is the creator of the legendary detective Sherlock Holmes?
questions.explanation: Sir Arthur Conan Doyle created Sherlock Holmes and Dr. John Watson in 1887.

questions.question: The prestigious Academy Awards are popularly known as what?
questions.explanation: The Academy Awards, or Oscars, honor excellence in the film industry.

questions.question: Which Disney movie features the character Queen Elsa of Arendelle?
questions.explanation: Elsa is the main character in Disney's Frozen franchise.

questions.question: Which fictional city does Batman protect?
questions.explanation: Gotham City is the dark, fictional metropolis that Batman calls home.

questions.question: What subject does Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry teach?
questions.explanation: Hogwarts is a school of magic for wizards and witches.

questions.question: In which film did the yellow Minions make their first appearance?
questions.explanation: Minions debuted in the 2010 animated film Despicable Me.

questions.question: The Joker is the arch-nemesis of which superhero?
questions.explanation: The Joker is Batman's most notorious criminal opponent in DC Comics.

quizzes.title: Science
quizzes.description: Explore the wonders of biology, chemistry, physics, astronomy, and units of measurement.

questions.question: What is the chemical symbol for Gold?
questions.explanation: The chemical symbol for gold is Au, derived from the Latin word 'aurum', meaning shining dawn.

questions.question: Which is the nearest star to Earth?
questions.explanation: The Sun is the closest star to Earth, situated at the center of our solar system.

questions.question: How many chambers does the human heart have?
questions.explanation: The human heart has four chambers: two upper atria and two lower ventricles.

questions.question: Which gas do plants primarily absorb during photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Plants take in carbon dioxide (CO2) and release oxygen during photosynthesis.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of speed?
questions.explanation: The SI unit of speed is meters per second (m/s).

questions.question: What does the first part of the word DNA stand for?
questions.explanation: DNA stands for Deoxyribonucleic Acid.

questions.question: What is the boiling point of pure water at sea level?
questions.explanation: Water boils at 100°C (212°F) under standard atmospheric pressure.

questions.question: Which planet in our solar system is known as the Red Planet?
questions.explanation: Mars is known as the Red Planet due to iron oxide (rust) on its surface.

questions.question: What is the largest organ of the human body?
questions.explanation: The skin is the body's largest organ, covering and protecting internal tissues.

questions.question: What is the standard unit of electric current?
questions.explanation: The Ampere (A) is the SI unit of electrical current.

quizzes.title: Sports
quizzes.description: Test your knowledge on soccer rules, historical events, badminton, cricket, and NBA facts.

questions.question: How many players are on the field for one football (soccer) team?
questions.explanation: A match is played by two teams, each with a maximum of 11 players including the goalkeeper.

questions.question: Every how many years are the Olympic Games celebrated?
questions.explanation: The Summer and Winter Olympics are held every four years.

questions.question: High-quality cricket bats are traditionally made from which wood?
questions.explanation: Cricket bats are made from English Willow (Salix alba caerulea) due to its strength and compression resistance.

questions.question: Which sport is played in the NBA?
questions.explanation: The NBA stands for National Basketball Association.

questions.question: Which tennis Grand Slam tournament is played on grass courts in the United Kingdom?
questions.explanation: Wimbledon is the oldest tennis tournament in the world and the only Grand Slam played on grass.

questions.question: How many chess pieces does each side start with in a standard chess game?
questions.explanation: Each player starts with 16 pieces: 1 King, 1 Queen, 2 Rooks, 2 Knights, 2 Bishops, and 8 Pawns.

questions.question: In badminton, what is the conical object that is hit back and forth commonly called?
questions.explanation: The badminton projectile is officially called a shuttlecock, but is commonly referred to as a birdie.

questions.question: How many active players per team are on court during a Kabaddi match?
questions.explanation: A Kabaddi match starts with seven players on court for each team.

questions.question: The FIFA World Cup is associated with which sport?
questions.explanation: FIFA stands for Federation Internationale de Football Association, organizing soccer's main event.

questions.question: Which cricket format typically allows the highest number of overall runs in a single match?
questions.explanation: Test cricket is played over five days with up to four innings, leading to high cumulative scores compared to short formats.

quizzes.title: JavaScript Advanced
quizzes.description: Test your mastery of closures, prototypes, event loop, hoisting, scopes, and advanced asynchronous JS execution patterns.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a native JavaScript data type?
questions.explanation: JavaScript only has a single 'Number' type (and 'BigInt') for numbers, unlike languages that distinguish floats and integers.

questions.question: What is the output of 'typeof null' in JavaScript?
questions.explanation: This is a historic bug in JavaScript, where null is mistakenly evaluated as having type 'object'.

questions.question: Which global JSON method is used to serialize an object into a JSON format string?
questions.explanation: JSON.stringify() converts a JavaScript value or object to a JSON string.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the 'use strict' directive in JavaScript?
options: To enforce stricter parsing and error handling
questions.correctAnswer: To enforce stricter parsing and error handling
questions.explanation: Strict mode catches common coding bloopers, throwing exceptions for unsafe actions like using undeclared variables.

questions.question: Which of the following keywords declares a block-scoped variable that can be re-assigned?
questions.explanation: 'let' is block-scoped and allows values to be updated, whereas 'const' prevents re-assignment and 'var' is function-scoped.

questions.question: What is a closures in JavaScript?
options: A method to close browser tabs
A function bundled together with references to its surrounding lexical environment
A tool to compress JS assets
questions.correctAnswer: A function bundled together with references to its surrounding lexical environment
questions.explanation: Closures allow nested inner functions to retain access to variables declared in parent scopes even after the outer function executes.

questions.question: Which company originally created and launched JavaScript in 1995?
questions.explanation: JavaScript was created by Brendan Eich at Netscape Communications under the initial name LiveScript.

questions.question: Which operator evaluates strict equality, checking both value and data type?
questions.explanation: The strict equality operator (===) compares both the value and type of two values without implicit type coercion.

questions.question: What is the resolution behavior of Promise.all()?
options: Rejects if any promise rejects, resolves only when all resolve
Resolves as soon as any input promise resolves
Resolves only if all input promises reject
questions.correctAnswer: Rejects if any promise rejects, resolves only when all resolve
questions.explanation: Promise.all resolves only when all promises resolve. If any promise rejects, it immediately rejects with that error.

questions.question: Which array method is used to accumulate and fold elements into a single aggregate value?
questions.explanation: The reduce() method executes a reducer function on each element, accumulating them into a single output value.

quizzes.title: World Geography
quizzes.description: Test your knowledge of capitals, landmarks, oceans, deserts, and general geographic trivia.

questions.question: Which is the longest river in the world?
questions.explanation: The Nile River is traditionally considered the longest river in the world, stretching over 6,650 kilometers.

questions.question: Which is the smallest independent sovereign country in the world?
questions.explanation: Vatican City is the smallest country in the world by both area (0.49 sq km) and population.

questions.question: What is the capital city of Australia?
questions.explanation: Canberra was selected as the capital in 1908 as a compromise between rivals Sydney and Melbourne.

questions.question: Which desert is the largest hot desert on Earth?
questions.explanation: The Sahara Desert is the largest hot desert, though the cold deserts of Antarctica and the Arctic are larger overall.

questions.question: Which country is home to the highest number of natural lakes?
questions.explanation: Canada has over 60% of the world's lakes, with lakes covering about 9% of the country's total area.

questions.question: What is the national capital of Canada?
questions.explanation: Ottawa was chosen as the capital of Canada by Queen Victoria in 1857 due to its strategic location.

questions.question: Which mountain range is traditionally considered the border between Europe and Asia?
questions.explanation: The Ural Mountains run north-south through western Russia and form the boundary between the two continents.

questions.question: Which is the largest island in the world that is not a continent?
questions.explanation: Greenland is the largest island in the world. Australia is classified as a continent rather than an island.

questions.question: Which nation is poetically referred to as the 'Land of the Rising Sun'?
questions.explanation: The name Japan translates literally to 'sun origin', giving rise to its famous nickname.

questions.question: Which is the highest uninterrupted waterfall in the world?
questions.explanation: Angel Falls in Venezuela has a height of 979 meters (3,212 feet) with an uninterrupted drop of 807 meters.
